result.summary: mdsol.com exposes a documented Medidata Rave Web Services HTTP API on tenant subdomains, with Basic auth and Medidata MAuth signing; Medidata also publishes a Python client package (`rwslib`) for that API.
result.description: Medidata provides software for clinical trial execution and data management, including the Rave electronic data capture platform. Its products support sponsors, CROs, sites, and related systems in managing clinical study data and operations.

medidata_basic.label: Medidata Rave user credentials
medidata_basic.setup: Join Medidata's Developer Central program via [Developer Central](https://www.medidata.com/en/clinical-trial-services/developer-central/) or the historical join path referenced in Medidata's blog ([Developer Central and the Path to Innovation](https://techblog.mdsol.com/2015/02/27/developer-central-path-to-innovation.html)). After registration and access approval, use your assigned Rave username and password for the Rave Web Services API over `HTTPS`.

medidata_mauth_app.label: Medidata MAuth application keypair
medidata_mauth_app.setup: Register for Medidata's developer program through [Developer Central](https://www.medidata.com/en/clinical-trial-services/developer-central/). Medidata documents an MAuth app model where you use an application UUID plus a private key to sign requests; Medidata's engineering blog shows this for iMedidata and notes Rave can be configured to recognize an MAuth app on behalf of a Rave user ([rwslib builders and requests-mauth Authentication](https://techblog.mdsol.com/2015/12/08/rwslib-and-mauth.html)). Obtain the app UUID and private key from Medidata as part of app registration/provisioning.

variables.name: subdomain
variables.description: Environment subdomain such as `innovate`, shown in Medidata examples as `https://innovate.mdsol.com/RaveWebServices`.
